   229→};
   230→
   231→/**
   232→ * Retrieves a value from a struct by a path, returning an `Effect`.
   233→ *
   234→ * Missing paths fail with {@link StructPathNotFoundError}. Found paths
   235→ * succeed with the value, including `value === undefined`.
   236→ *
   237→ * Supports a dual API:
   238→ * - Data-last: `dotGetEffect("attributes.name")(self)`
   239→ * - Data-first: `dotGetEffect(self, "attributes.name")`
   240→ * - Tuple paths: `dotGetEffect(["attributes", "name"] as const)(self)`
   241→ *
   242→ * @example
   243→ * ```ts
   244→ * import { dotGetEffect } from "@beep/utils"
   245→ * import { Effect } from "effect"
   246→ *
   247→ * const source = { attributes: { name: "beep" } } as const
   248→ *
   249→ * const program = dotGetEffect(source, "attributes.name").pipe(
   250→ *   Effect.catchTag("StructPathNotFoundError", (e) =>
   251→ *     Effect.succeed(`fallback: ${e.path}`)
   252→ *   )
   253→ * )
   254→ * ```
   255→ *
   256→ * @since 0.0.0
   257→ * @category getters
   258→ */
   259→export const dotGetEffect: {
   260→  <const P extends string>(
   261→    path: P
   262→  ): <Struct extends object>(
   263→    self: StructForPath<Struct, P>
   264→  ) => Effect.Effect<StructValueForPath<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   265→  <const P extends ReadonlyArray<string>>(
   266→    path: P
   267→  ): <Struct extends object>(
   268→    self: StructForPathTuple<Struct, P>
   269→  ) => Effect.Effect<StructValueForPathTuple<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   270→  <Struct extends object, const P extends StructPath<Struct>>(
   271→    self: Struct,
   272→    path: P
   273→  ): Effect.Effect<StructPathValue<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   274→  <Struct extends object, const P extends StructPathTuple<Struct>>(
   275→    self: Struct,
   276→    path: P
   277→  ): Effect.Effect<StructPathTupleValue<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   278→} = F.dual(
   279→  2,
   280→  <Struct extends object>(self: Struct, path: PathInput): Effect.Effect<unknown, StructPathNotFoundError> => {
   281→    const lookup = lookupAtPath(self, path);
   282→    if (!lookup.found) {
   283→      const pathStr = P.isString(path) ? path : F.pipe(path, A.join("."));
   284→      return Effect.fail(new StructPathNotFoundError({ path: pathStr }));
   285→    }
   286→    return Effect.succeed(lookup.value);
   287→  }
   288→) as {
   289→  <const P extends string>(
   290→    path: P
   291→  ): <Struct extends object>(
   292→    self: StructForPath<Struct, P>
   293→  ) => Effect.Effect<StructValueForPath<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   294→  <const P extends ReadonlyArray<string>>(
   295→    path: P
   296→  ): <Struct extends object>(
   297→    self: StructForPathTuple<Struct, P>
   298→  ) => Effect.Effect<StructValueForPathTuple<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   299→  <Struct extends object, const P extends StructPath<Struct>>(
   300→    self: Struct,
   301→    path: P
   302→  ): Effect.Effect<StructPathValue<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   303→  <Struct extends object, const P extends StructPathTuple<Struct>>(
   304→    self: Struct,
   305→    path: P
   306→  ): Effect.Effect<StructPathTupleValue<Struct, P>, StructPathNotFoundError>;
   307→};
